dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3870 IS: Lifelong Learning Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Peters (for himself, Mr. Budd, and Mr. Hickenlooper) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions

form.official-title: To amend the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act to permit greater flexibility in carrying out incumbent worker training programs, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Lifelong Learning Act.

section.header: Incumbent worker training and transitional jobs standard reservation of funds
section.text: Section 134(d) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3174(d)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (4)(A)(i), by striking 20 and inserting 30; and
in paragraph (5), in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking 10 and inserting 15.

section.text: Section 116(d)(2)(A) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3141(d)(2)(A)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking (A) information and inserting (A)(i) information;
in clause (i), as so designated, by adding and at the end; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: in the case of a State in which local areas are implementing incumbent worker training programs under section 134(d)(4), information on the levels of performance achieved for those programs with respect to the primary indicators of performance described in subsection (b)(2)(A), which information shall be used by the State and the Secretary of Labor in conjunction with the Secretary of Education to adapt the State adjusted levels of performance with respect to such indicators for the adult program and for the dislocated worker program authorized under chapter 3 of subtitle B.

section.header: Expanding the flexibility of one-stop operators
section.text: Section 121(d)(2) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3151(d)(2)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking ; and and inserting a semicolon;
in subparagraph (B)(vi), by striking the period and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: subject to approval from the chief elected official and Governor and in accordance with any other eligibility criteria established by the State, and notwithstanding subparagraph (B), may be a local board, if the local board—

clause.text: enters into a written agreement with the chief elected official and clarifies how the local board will carry out the functions and responsibilities of a one-stop operator in a manner that complies with appropriate internal controls to prevent any conflicts of interest, which shall include how the local board, while serving as a one-stop operator, will comply with—

subclause.text: the relevant Office of Management and Budget circulars relating to conflicts of interest; and
any applicable State conflict of interest policy; and

clause.text: complies with the other applicable requirements of this subsection.
